description: Trigger and behavior cases for the framework-agnostic common control set: map-don't-rebuild, seven domains, framework-neutral text, honest status, controlled-document handling.

cases.prompt: We're going for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 next year, and ISO 42001 after that. Build us a common control set so we don't maintain three separate control lists.

assertions: Produces ONE framework-agnostic catalog across the seven domains (plus AI governance for 42001), not per-framework lists.
Maps existing mechanisms to shipped artifacts by name (authorization matrix, RLS audit, audit log, incident runbook, supply-chain review) instead of re-specifying them.
Gives every control an ID, objective, named owner, mechanism or MISSING, evidence hook, and implemented/partial/missing status.
Keeps framework clause/criteria citations out of the catalog, deferring them to multi-framework-crosswalk.

cases.prompt: We already have solid security engineering — RLS audits, an authorization matrix, incident runbooks — but no compliance program. What does our control baseline actually look like?

assertions: Inventories the existing artifacts first and maps them as implemented controls rather than starting from a blank framework template.
Carries known residue honestly (e.g. encryption design review, security-event alerting, access recertification) as partial/missing instead of claiming full coverage.
Marks net-new needs (risk register, access reviews, vendor assessments) as missing controls with owners to assign.

cases.prompt: Design our ISO 27001 ISMS — clauses 4 through 10, internal audit program, and management review cadence.

assertions: Management-system design for one framework is iso-27001-isms-architect; the foundation only supplies the control catalog it consumes.

cases.prompt: Write the common control set and annotate each control with its ISO 27001 Annex A number and SOC 2 criteria reference so it's all in one file.

assertions: Declines to embed framework clause/criteria citations in the catalog and routes reference mapping to multi-framework-crosswalk.
Explains the drift risk: edition changes would rewrite the catalog, and Annex A identifiers must be verified against the licensed standard text, not asserted from memory.
Still delivers the framework-neutral catalog itself.
